title: monardella sinuata subsp. gerryi
intro: Monardella sinuata subsp. gerryi, also known as Coastal monardella, is a rare and endemic plant species native to California, United States. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae, which includes mint, basil, and rosemary. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The herbal preparations of Monardella sinuata subsp. gerryi include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, and may also provide antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits.
